slides.title: TCP and UDP checksum calculations for ILNP
Ericsson's Proposal for Key Management for DTLS Chunk
User Ports and Port Identifiers for Experiments
Export of ECN Information in IPFIX

polls.text: I have read/reviewed some version of draft-porfiri-tsvwg-sctp-dtls-handshake
Should we park l4sops until the SRM draft can be considered?

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Scanned the IESG objections. I don't think the discussion in the room reflected the IESG points.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: In particular Tommy Jensen's objections.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: I believe we would need to walk through the objections and come up with possible explanations. This is the job the wg has now.

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: Having port reviewers directly involved in revising the draft  would be good.  I suspect that the IESG did not understand the utility of the draft to the port reviewers.

chat.author: Wesley Eddy
chat.text: @harald -- it was not covered in detail but was compressed to 1 sentence on the use of PExIDs, and suggestion to work through an example

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: good point David.. the issue here is that only the authors are involved with IESG during the IESG review. Wes is also a port reviewer now adding him as editor would give two port reviewers to atted the IESG review..

chat.author: David Black
chat.text: @Zahed - +1, please also consider a port reviewer as draft shepherd.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: we will thanks for that, David.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: @Khloe I refused your slide-sharing request; if you meant to raise your hand, try again

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: so we've known that PIE is better than CoDel for 10 years, and only now get around to documenting it in an RFC? Seems a bit sad.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8033

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: thats your pie.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: here we are talking about FQ-PIE :-)

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: FQ-PIE implemented in Linux kernel since 2020. I thought the 10 year old white paper mentioned at the mike was also FQ-PIE, but it might have been just PIE. So only 6 years.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Correction: The default channel count in the spec for Webrtc is 64K. For the last 10 years the advertised value in libwebrtc has been 1024.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Fixed now, but it's not reasonable to omit it.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: If the parameters that need to be declared are fixed, duing them semantically seems fine.  If this is an SCTP extension point and more cdan be added in the future, I'd be more reluctant because the SDP would need to be chasing future SCTP extensions.  I don't know enough about SCTP to know for sure.

chat.author: Philipp Hancke
chat.text: the extension point in SCTP is the list of extensions which are as Michael said numbers so we might be able to avoid mapping them to sdp names. I'll take a look!

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Martin: the thing that WebTransport doesn't (yet) have is p2p.  And I suspect that making it p2p will mean that it's not 1 rtt.

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: I'm pleased to see this draft. Dropped out of queue as I will raise my comments to the list.
